questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two guns situated on the top of a hill of height 10 m fire one shot each with the same speed $5 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ at some interval of time. One gun fires horizontally and other fires upwards at an angle of $60^{\circ}$ with the horizontal. The shots collide at point $P$. The coordinates of point $P$ with respect to the bottom of the hill are (in metres $x, y$ ). find $\sqrt{3} x+y$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & x=5 \sqrt{3} t_1 \quad=5 \sqrt{3} \cos 60^{\circ} t_2, t_2=2 t_1 \\ & 10-y=\frac{1}{2} g t_1^2 \quad-5 \sqrt{3} \sin 30^{\circ} t_2+\frac{1}{2} g t_2^2 \quad \text { Solving } \\ & t_2=2 t_1=2 \mathrm{~s} \quad \therefore x=5 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m} \quad y=5 \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: Two points, $A$ and $B$, are located on the ground a certain distance $d=10 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m}$ apart. Two rocks are launched simultaneously from points A and B with equal speeds but at different angles. Each rock lands at the launch point of the other. Knowing that one of the rocks is launched at an angle $\theta=37^{\circ}$ with the horizontal, what is the minimum distance between the rocks during the flight? (given $\cos 37^{\circ}=3 / 5$)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle is projected horizontally with velocity $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ from an inclined plane. Incline plane starts moving with acceleration $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ vertically upward as shown. The time (in second) after which particle will land on the plane is given by $\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \mathrm{~s}$. Find the value of $\mathrm{n} .\left(\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right)$
questions.solution: Acceleration of ball w.r.t. plane $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{bp}}=\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{bg}}-\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{ps}}=2 \mathrm{~g} \text { (downward) } \\ & \mathrm{s}=\mathrm{ut}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{at}^2 \Rightarrow 0=\left(10 \sin 30^{\circ}\right) \mathrm{t}-\frac{1}{2} \times\left(2 \mathrm{~g} \cos 30^{\circ}\right) \mathrm{t}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=1 / \mathrm{O} 3 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle is projected from ground towards a vertical wall 80 m away at an angle of $37^{\circ}$ with horizontal with initial velocity of $50 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. After its collision with wall \& then once with ground find at what distance in meter from wall will it strike the ground. The component of velocity normal to the surface becomes half after collision with each surface.
questions.solution: After first collision : $\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{y}}=30-\mathrm{gt} ; \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{x}}=-20 \hat{i} ; t_1=\frac{80}{40}=2 \mathrm{sec} ; \mathrm{t}_2=\mathrm{T}-\mathrm{t}_1=4 \mathrm{sec}$ Before second collision : $v_x=-20 \hat{i} ; \mathrm{x}=20 \times 4=-80 \mathrm{~m} ; \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{y}}=10-10\left(\mathrm{t}_2\right)=-30 \hat{j}$ After second collision : $v_x=-20 \hat{i} ; v_y=+15 \hat{j} \quad$ Range $=60 \mathrm{~m}$ Net: $60 \mathrm{~m}+80 \mathrm{~m}=140 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A projectile launched from the ground explodes into three fragments of equal mass at the top point of the trajectory. One of the fragments lands $t$ seconds after the explosion two other fragments land simultaneously 2 t seconds after the explosion. How high above the ground does the projectile explode? (take $\mathrm{t}=2 / 5$ seconds and $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution:
Let $u s$ call $h$ the height above the ground at which the projectile explodes and $v_{k y}(j=1,2,3)$ the $y$ component of the velocity at the moment of the explosion for the three fragments. I will label with 1 the fragment which lands after $t$ seconds.
Since the landing time depends only on the $y$-component of the velocity at the moment of the explosion, we have: $v_{3 y}=v_{2 y}$. We then need only the equations for fragments 1 and 2 , stating that at landing time the $y$-coordinate will be equal to 0 :
$h+v_{1 y} t-\frac{g^2}{2}=0$
$h+v_{2 y} t-\frac{g(2 t)^2}{2}=0$
Since the masses of the three fragments are equal, and the y -component of the projectile momentum at the moment of the explosion is equal to 0 (it explodes at the top of the trajectory), the momentum conservation law reads:
$v_{1 y}+v_{2 y}+v_{3 y}=0$
The solution of the above equations is then:
$v_{1 y}=\frac{-3}{4} \mathrm{gt}$
$\mathrm{v}_{2 \mathrm{y}}=\mathrm{v}_{3 \mathrm{y}}=\frac{3}{8} \mathrm{gt}$
The height above the ground at which the projectile explodes is then: $\mathrm{h}=5 \mathrm{gt}^2 / 4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle is moving in a circle of radius R in such a way that at any instant the normal and the tangential component of its acceleration are equal. If its speed at $t=0$ is $v_0$ then the time it takes to complete the first revolution is $\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\alpha v_0}\left(1-e^{-\beta \pi}\right)$. Find the value of $(\alpha+\beta)^6$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{d v}{d t}=\frac{v^2}{R} \Rightarrow \int_{v_0}^v \frac{d v}{v^2}=\frac{1}{R} \int_0^t d t \Rightarrow\left(-\frac{1}{v}\right)_{v_0}^v=\frac{t}{R} \Rightarrow v=\frac{v_0}{1-\frac{v_0}{R} t} \Rightarrow \frac{d s}{d t}=\frac{v_0}{1-\frac{v_0}{R} t} \Rightarrow \int_0^{2 \pi R} d s=\int_0^t \frac{v_0}{\left(1-\frac{v_0}{R} t\right)} d t \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \pi R=-R\left[\ln \left(1-\frac{v_0}{R} t\right)\right]_0^t \Rightarrow 2 \pi=-\ln \left(1-\frac{v_0}{R} t\right) \Rightarrow 1-\frac{v_0}{R} t=e^{-2 \pi} \Rightarrow t=\frac{R}{v_0}\left(1-e^{-2 \pi}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow a=1, \beta=2 \Rightarrow(\alpha+\beta)^6=(1+2)^6=729\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle of mass $m$ is moving with constant speed in a vertical circle in $\mathrm{X}-\mathrm{Z}$ plane. There is a small bulb at some distance on Z -axis. The maximum distance of the shadow of the particle on X -axis is found $\alpha\left(\frac{25}{24}\right)$. Find the value of $\alpha$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{d x}{d t}=v_0, \frac{d y}{d t}=a \omega \cos \omega t \Rightarrow x=v_0 t, y=a \sin \omega t \\ & \Rightarrow y=\operatorname{asin}\left(\frac{\omega x}{v_0}\right) \Rightarrow m=1, n=1 \Rightarrow(m+2 n)^2=(1+2)^2=9\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A bead moves along straight horizontal wire of length $L$, starting from the left end with a velocity $v_0$. Its retardation is proportional to the distance from the right end of the wire. Find the initial retardation (in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$) (at left end of the wire) if the bead reaches the right end of the wire with a velocity $\frac{v_0}{2}$. (given $v_0=5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $L=1 \mathrm{~m}$ )
questions.solution: $\mathrm{v} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\mathrm{k}(\mathrm{L}-\mathrm{x})$, where k is a constant. $\begin{aligned} & \int_{v_0}^{v_0 / 2} v d v=-k \int_0^L(L-x) d x \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{3}{4} v_0^2=K L^2 \end{aligned}$ So initial retardation $=\mathrm{KL}=\frac{3}{4} \frac{\mathrm{v}_0^2}{\mathrm{~L}}=\frac{3}{4} \times 25=18.75 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
